$schema_note: Iter-9 backfill snapshot fixture (see us-ecommerce-pmi-2026q1.json note). Source-of-record for inventory_carrying inputs cited by domestic ecommerce profiles. Profile inputs MIRROR this file's data block. This file ALSO serves as the offline DECLARED fallback for inventory_carrying.isr_observed when us-ecommerce-live.json's live FRED ISRATIO bind is unavailable and the cache_policy authorises stale data.
applies_to_note: US ecommerce. ISR observed 1.62 vs historic mean 1.45 indicates a mild glut (catalog b.2: >1.6 = gluts). dio_sensitivity=1.0 placeholder.

source.citation: Synthetic Iter-7 fixture; ISR 1.62 vs historic mean 1.45 (mild glut). Closed form SIGNED OFF as Iter-7 v1 baseline (see backend/components/inventory_carrying.py); CCC-style (DIO+DSO-DPO) deferred. Spec per design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md b.2.
source.retrieval_method: fabricated for the Iter-7 composer test. In Iter-9 the LIVE path binds isr_observed to FRED ISRATIO via us-ecommerce-live.json; this fixture is the offline declared value + authorised-stale fallback.
source.live_binding_target: FRED ISRATIO (Iter-9 live bind, value_scale=1.0 as-is) + Census M3/MRTS corroboration (deferred, unverified query shape). NOTE: 'ISRATIO' is bound per the design docs verbatim; verify the returned magnitude on the Windows smoke (total-business vs retailers'-specific series).

_meta.iteration_authored: Iter-9 (backfill; values mirror us-ecommerce.json Iter-7 inline inputs)
